title: Field Operations Guides (FOGs)
raw_text: Home About Resources NCSWIC Updates Field Operations Guides (FOGs) Related topics: Emergency Communications , Emergency Communications Planning Field Operations Guides (FOGs) are technical references for emergency communications planning and for radio technicians responsible for radios that will be used in emergency responses. National Interoperability Field Operations Guide (NIFOG) National Interoperability Field Operations Guide Version 2.02  (.pdf, 4.17 MB) (2025) The NIFOG is a technical reference for emergency communications planning and for radio technicians responsible for radios that will be used in disaster response. The NIFOG includes rules and regulations for use of nationwide and other interoperability channels, tables of frequencies and standard channel names, and other reference material, formatted as a pocket-sized guide for radio technicians to carry with them. New content in 2.02 includes updated contact information. National Interoperability Field Operations Guide Fact Sheet  (.pdf, 410 KB) (2025) Public Safety Library Mobile App For more information on how to download the app, please visit the Public Safety Library Mobile App page . Programming Guides Programming Guide for Interoperability Radio Channels  (.pdf, 97.91 KB) (2021) (*) This guide is intended for radio system managers and technicians responsible for designing and programming interoperability channels for public safety radios in their community. Programming Template for Interoperability Radio Channels  (.pdf, 259.36 KB) (2021) (*) The template is a series Communications Resource Availability Worksheets to assist the user in effectively managing the Nationwide Interoperability Channels. (*) Denotes Archived Material: In an effort to keep CISA.gov current, the archive contains information that may not reflect current policy or programs. Didn't find what you're looking for?   Check out the SAFECOM Resource Library . An alphabetized list of hyperlinks to all SAFECOM, NCSWIC, FPIC, and Project 25 (P25) resources.
summary: 基于摘要信息。美国网络安全和基础设施安全局（CISA）发布了《国家互操作性现场操作指南》（NIFOG）2.02版，这是一份面向应急通信规划人员和无线电技术人员的技术参考手册，旨在为灾害响应中的无线电使用提供标准化指导。该更新虽为常规修订，但作为官方技术标准，对确保应急通信设备的互操作性和合规性具有基础性作用。
key_points: NIFOG 2.02版更新了联系信息，保持技术参考的时效性。
指南包含全国互操作性频道的使用规则、频率表和标准频道名称。
旨在帮助无线电技术人员在灾害响应中快速配置和操作设备。
相关编程指南和模板（2021年）已归档，建议参考最新资源。
innovation: 本文未涉及AI、模型能力或技术框架的突破性进展。NIFOG的核心价值在于标准化和流程优化，而非技术创新。它通过提供统一的频率分配、频道命名规则和操作规范，确保不同机构（如警察、消防、医疗）在应急响应中能使用兼容的无线电设备进行通信。这解决了互操作性（Interoperability）问题，即不同系统间无缝交换信息的能力。指南以口袋书形式设计，便于现场技术人员快速查阅，减少了配置错误和通信延迟。虽然内容基于传统无线电技术，但标准化是构建可靠应急通信网络的基础，尤其在复杂灾害场景中至关重要。
takeaway: 对灾后通信恢复业务，NIFOG提供了直接的现场操作参考，帮助技术人员快速部署和调试无线电设备，确保跨部门通信畅通。它间接支持重大场合通信保障，通过标准化频道管理，减少干扰并提高协调效率。 近期（6个月），应急机构可基于此指南更新培训材料和设备配置；中期（1-2年），可能影响采购决策，优先选择兼容NIFOG标准的设备；长期（3年以上），有助于推动全国性互操作性协议的完善。 建议跟踪：1. 定期检查CISA官网以获取NIFOG的后续更新版本；2. 将NIFOG内容整合到本地应急通信培训课程中；3. 评估现有无线电设备是否符合指南中的频率和频道标准，必要时进行升级或调整。
non_expert_explanation: 这件事意味着应急响应人员（如消防员、警察）在灾害现场使用无线电时，有了最新的官方操作手册，能更快地设置设备并确保不同团队之间通话顺畅，提高救援效率。

title: Smarter, faster, safer: How AI is reinventing the emergency ...
raw_text: ## Assisting from call to closure An automobile accident, personal injury at home or missing child scenario doesn’t end once a unit is dispatched. In most instances, telecommunicators remain on the line as situations evolve, and Prepared continues to assist by capturing additional information in real time. “We have the ability to get live video from the caller themselves, which can help with situational awareness and catch things that maybe they’re not verbally reporting,” said Madeline Mariuzza, vice president of customer experience at Prepared. “That can help a lot with officer safety and also as the incident progresses. [...] “One of the big things is awareness of what’s going on in the room,” he said. “The system is smart enough to alert us to keywords, so if one of our call takers is providing CPR instructions to a child, that’s a very traumatic call. We want to make sure they’re providing the proper instructions, so the supervisor can click on that call and make sure the proper protocols are being followed without standing over their shoulder. But for our own mental health and our own employees, we can also go over to them after the call and make sure they’re OK, see if they need a break and provide them with some guidance as well.” ## Connecting beyond the telephone [...] ## From confusion to clarity A difficult situation that many emergency call takers have experienced might sound like this: “911, where’s your emergency?” Instead of a clear response, there’s wind in the background, panicked breathing and a few unclear words – maybe “road,” maybe “home.” You ask the caller to slow down and repeat themselves. More muffled words follow. “Alley.” Or was it “valley”? After several attempts, the caller finally communicates a clear location – the alley behind Market Street Grocery.
normalized_title: smarter faster safer how ai is reinventing the emergency
summary: 基于摘要信息，美国应急响应技术公司Prepared展示了其AI系统在911呼叫处理中的实际应用。该系统通过实时视频分析、关键词检测和语音识别技术，提升现场态势感知和调度员支持能力。这标志着AI正从实验室走向应急通信一线，直接影响呼叫中心运营效率和应急响应质量。
key_points: AI系统可实时分析911呼叫视频，增强现场态势感知
关键词检测功能自动标记高风险呼叫，支持督导员介入
语音识别技术帮助解析模糊语音，加速位置信息获取
innovation: 该AI系统的核心创新在于将多模态感知与实时决策支持结合。系统通过计算机视觉分析实时视频流，识别现场环境细节（如武器、伤者位置）；同时运用自然语言处理进行语音识别和关键词检测，当检测到'CPR'等关键词时自动提醒督导员。与传统的语音记录回放相比，这种实时分析能力使调度中心能在事件演进过程中主动干预，而非事后复盘。技术原理上，系统采用了端到端的AI管道：前端设备采集音视频数据，云端AI模型进行实时分析，结果通过低延迟接口推送给调度员界面。
takeaway: 对应急通信业务而言，这种AI增强的呼叫处理系统直接提升了灾后通信恢复和重大活动保障中的信息获取效率。在混乱的应急场景中，系统能帮助调度员更快理解现场状况，减少信息传递延迟，为指挥决策提供更准确的一线情报。 近期（6个月内），已有Prepared等公司推出商用产品，表明技术达到产品可用阶段；中期（1-2年），随着更多911中心采购，可能形成行业标准；长期（3年以上），AI与5G、物联网的融合将实现全自动的态势感知与资源调度。 建议跟踪：1. 调研Prepared等公司的产品部署案例和客户反馈；2. 评估将类似AI功能集成到现有应急通信平台的可行性；3. 关注美国NENA（国家紧急号码协会）等机构是否会制定相关技术标准。
non_expert_explanation: 这意味着未来拨打911时，AI能帮助接线员更快理解你的处境——即使你惊慌失措说不清话，系统也能从背景声音和视频中识别关键信息，让救援更快到达正确地点。
